identitySentence: Caitlyn Ashley Law is a Denton estate planning, probate, and guardianship firm founded in March 2021 by Caitlyn Ashley, an attorney whose Denton roots run five generations deep.

concept.headline: Estate planning in Denton that answers life's what-ifs, starting with one simple step
concept.subheadline: Thinking about a will, a trust, probate, or guardianship for someone you love can feel overwhelming. Caitlyn Ashley Law helps Denton families put a clear plan in place, and now reaching out takes minutes instead of a phone call during office hours.
credibilityPoints: Practicing law since 2018; opened Caitlyn Ashley Law in Denton in March 2021
Former lead probate attorney at Leigh Hilton PLLC in Denton
Super Lawyers Rising Stars, 2023-2026; Best of Denton County 2023 winner for Wills & Estate Planning Attorney
Denton County Bar Association member with five-generation Denton roots

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: From simple wills to trusts, powers of attorney, and special needs trusts, Caitlyn Ashley Law builds estate plans around your family's specific needs. The firm also guides families through the probate process and helps when guardianship becomes necessary for an aging or disabled loved one.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: Caitlyn Ashley's Denton roots run five generations deep, and her office sits at 620 Bolivar Street in the heart of Denton. She founded her own firm here in March 2021 after serving as lead probate attorney at a Denton firm.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: Consultations are by appointment, Monday through Thursday 9 to 5 and Friday 9 to noon. You will talk through your family, your assets, and what you want protected, and you will leave knowing your honest options and next steps.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

Dear Caitlyn Ashley and the Caitlyn Ashley Law team,

Your firm has earned real recognition in a short time: a Best of Denton County 2023 win in the Wills & Estate Planning Attorney category and Super Lawyers Rising Stars selections from 2023 through 2026. Your website already does a lot right, too. It is modern, mobile-friendly, and speaks to families in plain, compassionate language about life's what-ifs.

One thing it does not yet do is let a potential client take the next step the moment they are ready. Today, reaching Caitlyn Ashley Law means a phone call during by-appointment hours or a general contact form, and every inquiry waits for a manual reply. For a solo estate planning, probate, and guardianship practice, the after-hours moments matter most: a daughter worrying about guardianship for a parent, or a spouse who has just lost someone and now faces probate. Those visitors deserve a fast, specific response even when you are with a client or in court.

That is why we built a Lead Capturer for Caitlyn Ashley Law, with its own identity and a custom contact form written for your practice:

"Caitlyn Ashley Law is a Denton estate planning, probate, and guardianship firm founded in March 2021 by Caitlyn Ashley, an attorney whose Denton roots run five generations deep."

Here is how it works:
1. A potential client submits the form, and the inquiry is emailed directly to your firm's inbox. There is no new dashboard to remember to check.
2. The potential client automatically receives a response specific to what they wrote about, whether that is estate planning, probate, or guardianship, not a generic acknowledgment.
3. That response presents your genuinely available consultation times.
4. A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so the consultations on your calendar are committed ones.

We would like you to test the complete experience yourself before deciding anything. The Lead Capturer runs as a 15-day proof period: if it does not earn its place in your practice, you walk away.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):
1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email address you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject line identifies Caitlyn Ashley Law and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To address contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to your TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm that response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer email account actually receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual topic of the inquiry.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter type.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails. Contact us and we will make it right.

No pressure and no obligation: just 15 days, 1 demo, and proof you can see in your own inbox.

Respectfully,
The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]
If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
